# Reproducer derived from https://github.com/benoitc/gunicorn/issues/2164
# repo: benoitc/gunicorn
# title: RecursionError in v20.0.0 from Docker

# Last frames in traceback:
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/base_async.py line 111 in handle_request
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/http/wsgi.py line 397 in write_file
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/http/wsgi.py line 387 in sendfile
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/ggevent.py line 36 in _gevent_sendfile
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/ggevent.py line 36 in _gevent_sendfile
#   /usr/local/lib/python3.7/site-packages/gunicorn/workers/ggevent.py line 36 in _gevent_sendfile

# Minimal repro (conceptual):

def repro():
    # Conditions described in the issue:
    # I'm developed several python web services from Docker-compose, I'm using following Docker images:
    # * python:3.7
    # *  nginx:1.13.3
    # So I'm create two services (containers) that communicates between them.
    # We have no errors until today, and I think that the error is the version of gunicorn that in my requirements.txt is bein
    # So in the latest days, after remove containers and images, it was installed the latest version of gunicorn (that's
    raise RecursionError('reproduce: see https://github.com/benoitc/gunicorn/issues/2164')

if __name__ == '__main__':
    repro()
expected_fix_hint: Convert to iterative; increase sys.setrecursionlimit guardedly.
